summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete. Added 25 new pytest tests for PATCH /api/messages/{id}, DELETE /api/messages/{id}, DELETE /api/chats/{id}, PATCH /api/me and their WS broadcasts. 25/25 new tests PASS; full regression 62/62 PASS (100%). All new endpoints: auth-gated (401), sender/member ownership enforced (403/404), MongoDB _id excluded, edited_at exposed on serializer, chat preview recomputed correctly on message delete/edit, group-leave vs full-delete branching works, and all four expected WS events verified live: message_updated, message_deleted, chat_deleted, profile_updated.

minor.issue: picture accepts arbitrary string with no size/format validation. A malicious client could store a multi-MB base64 blob which then gets echoed in _clean_user() everywhere the user appears (chat lists, search, presence broadcasts). Consider capping length (e.g. 400 KB) or validating data URL prefix.

When a group member leaves, no system/informational message is stored in the chat. Remaining members only get a 'chat_deleted' WS event (which semantically means 'the chat is gone'), which is misleading — from their POV the chat still exists, one member just left. A distinct WS type like 'chat_member_left' would be more accurate.

No lock on chat_id — if the chat has since been deleted (e.g. via DELETE /api/chats/{id}) but the message somehow survives, the code will crash on chat.get(...). Currently unreachable because delete_chat cascades messages, but worth a defensive check.

action_items: Optional hardening: validate /api/me picture payload (size + data URL prefix) before persistence.
Optional UX: consider a separate WS event type for 'member left a group' vs full chat deletion so peers can render the correct in-chat notice.
Existing iter-1 backlog: dead GET /api/auth/me handler still present.
critical_code_review_comments: edit_message correctly refreshes chat.last_message only when the edited msg is BOTH sent by the current user AND is the newest by created_at — logic verified. However, if the newest msg was authored by someone else and the edit is on an older own msg, no preview change happens (correct behaviour, but worth documenting).

delete_message re-computes last_message_at from the surviving newest message. This means the chat can move UP or DOWN in the list-sorted-by-last_message_at after a delete, which is desirable, but the /api/chats list is sorted server-side once — clients relying on WS updates need to re-sort locally when they see message_deleted.

delete_chat broadcasts 'chat_deleted' to ALL previous members regardless of the leave-vs-delete branch. That means when u1 leaves a 3-person group, u2 and u3 receive 'chat_deleted' even though the chat still exists for them. See minor issue above.
update_me only broadcasts to contacts (users sharing at least one chat). A newly-registered user with no chats yet gets no WS fan-out — correct.
PATCH /api/me: patch dict is built conditionally; passing {} makes it a no-op DB call — fine. Empty-string name is silently ignored (not an error). Documented behaviour.
seed_data_creation: Reuses conftest.py session-scoped seed fixture (TEST_alice/bob/carol users + sessions). Each test creates its own chats/messages tagged with 'TEST' text; conftest teardown removes chats+messages tied to test users at session end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: https://chat-kotlin-react.preview.emergentagent.com. All 62 backend tests green. Run full regression via `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. New iter-3 endpoints exercised: PATCH /api/messages/{id} (403 non-sender, 400 empty, 404 missing, last-msg preview refresh), DELETE /api/messages/{id} (403 non-sender, preview recompute, null preview when chat empty), DELETE /api/chats/{id} (404 non-member, full-delete cascade for 1-on-1 and last-member group, leave-behaviour for group with others), PATCH /api/me (401, name trim+80-cap, empty ignored, picture set). WS broadcasts verified live: message_updated, message_deleted, chat_deleted (with 'by' field), profile_updated (only to contacts sharing a chat). Regression: message serializer now emits edited_at (None on fresh sends).
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All spec'd iter-3 behaviours verified end-to-end (REST + WS + DB state).
